category|Category:Kashikuri-jinja}} [[JA:加紫久利神社]] [[SIMPLE:Kashikuri Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q11398800}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 加紫久利神社 |画像 = [[File:Kashikuri shrine.jpg|270px|加紫久利神社]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|鹿児島県|Q15701}}{{jalink|出水市|Q15734}}下鯖町1272 |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}
{{jalink|多紀理毘売命}}
{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013}}
{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}
{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = 伝{{jalink|神代|Q3577055}}
伝{{jalink|大宝 (日本)|Q1346269|大宝}}２年（{{jalink|702年|Q23497}}） |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|3月4日|Q2396}}・{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}} |神事 = 種子蒔祭・牛舞（3月4日） |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''加紫久利神社'''（かしくりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|鹿児島県|Q15701}}{{jalink|出水市|Q15734}}下鯖町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}} *{{jalink|多紀理毘売命}}　※加紫久利神社社伝によると天照大神の娘とされる。 *{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013}} *{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} *{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}} また、{{jalink|摂末社|Q1071160}}として{{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665}}が祭られている。 === 沿革 === 創始年代は不詳であるが、社伝によれば遠く{{jalink|神代|Q3577055}}とも創建以来凡そ2000年ともされているが、『{{jalink|式内社調査報告}}』によると、当神社は、{{jalink|大宝 (日本)|Q1346269|大宝}}2年（{{jalink|702年|Q23497}}）{{jalink|薩摩|Q227481}}建国と同時に{{jalink|肥後|Q1618006}}国境にそびえる{{jalink|矢筈岳 (熊本県水俣市・鹿児島県出水市)|Q11584247|加紫久利山}}の山麓に{{jalink|宇佐|Q22128861}}の３女神を移祭したものであり、それは{{jalink|大宝 (日本)|Q1346269|大宝}}2年（{{jalink|702年|Q23497}}）の{{jalink|隼人|Q1151764}}征討軍に参加した{{jalink|豊前|Q411745}}{{jalink|上毛郡|Q10867537|上三毛郡}}加自久也里出身地と推定される加志君和多利が、その出身地の{{jalink|大富神社|Q11434509}}の祭神を奉祀したものであるという。{{jalink|隼人|Q1151764}}征討軍に従軍して功績のあった加志君は、戦後{{jalink|肥後|Q1618006}}国境守備を命ぜられてここに屯住し、郷里加自久也里の地名にちなんでこの地を加自久里と呼んだとされる。当時{{jalink|豊前|Q411745}}加自久也里は{{jalink|郷里制|Q10561865}}の改正で炊江郷となるが、{{jalink|薩摩|Q227481}}加自久里も本家分家の関係から借家郷という訓の同じ郷名に替えて{{jalink|和名抄}}にその名をとどめることになったとされる『角川日本地名大辞典４６鹿児島県』。 {{jalink|弘仁|Q1373070}}14年（{{jalink|823年|Q29944}}）に日本66国のうちの{{jalink|薩摩|Q227481}}のための大社として加紫久利神社が確定したという。 {{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}元年（{{jalink|851年|Q29966}}）に{{jalink|官社}}に列し『{{jalink|文徳天皇実録}}』仁寿元年6月戊午（17日）条。、{{jalink|貞観_(日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}2年（{{jalink|860年|Q24714}}）3月に従五位下から従五位上に昇った『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』貞観2年3月20日条。。 『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では{{jalink|枚聞神社|Q3136198}}と共に名前が見え、{{jalink|薩摩国|Q667475}}の神社として記載のあるただ2つの例の一つであった。そのため後世「薩摩二ノ宮」とも言われている（薩摩国は{{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}以後、{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}が2社存在し{{jalink|一宮#二宮、三宮|Q1656379|二宮}}不詳）。 当神社の由緒にある{{jalink|矢筈岳 (熊本県水俣市・鹿児島県出水市)|Q11584247|加紫久利山}}は、{{jalink|熊本県|Q130308}}{{jalink|水俣市|Q386110}}と{{jalink|鹿児島県|Q15701}}{{jalink|出水市|Q15734}}の県境にまたがる{{jalink|矢筈岳 (熊本県水俣市・鹿児島県出水市)|Q11584247|矢筈岳}}の古い呼び名であるとされ、山頂には'''矢越神社'''が鎮座しており当神社が管理している。この神社の例祭が当神社の春祭と同じ３月４日であることから両社の間には何らかの関係があったとも考えられるが、現在しかとした伝承は伝わっていないものの、元々は{{jalink|矢筈岳 (熊本県水俣市・鹿児島県出水市)|Q11584247|加紫久利山}}を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}とする{{jalink|山岳信仰|Q93697439}}から始まった神社であったとも考えられる。 {{jalink|薩摩|Q227481}}建国にゆかりをもつ歴史のある神社であることから、その後この地の支配者となった{{jalink|島津氏|Q1061406}}の尊崇も篤く、21代{{jalink|島津吉貴|Q11246537}}は、当神社を{{jalink|薩摩国|Q667475|薩州}}の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129}}と唱えるよう迎出して社殿の造営に当たった。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}10年（{{jalink|1877年|Q7773}}）の{{jalink|西南戦争|Q714820}}により全焼し、社殿はおろか歴代伝えた宝物すら失った。同13年（{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}）に社殿は再興されたものの、その後の新田開発などで境内を大きく浸食され、{{jalink|戦後|Q1201472}}には社殿崩壊の危機にまで瀕した。 === 社殿 === 現在の社殿は地元有志の寄付により{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}36年（{{jalink|1961年|Q3696}}）に再建され、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}元年（{{jalink|1989年|Q2425}}）に改築されたものである。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === *『角川日本地名大辞典４６鹿児島県』、{{jalink|角川書店|Q1054765}}、昭和58年 === 外部リンク === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ 加紫久利神社] - 鹿児島県神社庁 {{神道 横}} category|Category:Kashikuri-jinja}} [[JA:加紫久利神社]] [[SIMPLE:Kashikuri Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q11398800}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kashikuri Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kashikuri shrine.jpg|270px|Kashikuri Shrine]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}
{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建 = Trad. {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}}
Trad. {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|March 4|Q2396}} / {{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and its former rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} In addition, {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{jalink|sessha/massha|Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{jalink|Usa|Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{jalink|Buzen|Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{jalink|Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{jalink|Kōnin|Q1373070}} 14 ({{jalink|823|Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{jalink|official shrine}} in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}})'{{jalink|Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{jalink|Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}}, leaving {{jalink|ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{jalink|Minamata|Q386110}}, {{jalink|Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308}}, and {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{jalink|mountain worship|Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{jalink|Shimazu clan|Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{jalink|Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} of {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475|Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{jalink|Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 36 ({{jalink|1961|Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 1 ({{jalink|1989|Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

shrine.jpg|270px|Kashikuri Shrine]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}
{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建 = Trad. {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}}
Trad. {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|March 4|Q2396}} / {{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and its former rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} In addition, {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{jalink|sessha/massha|Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{jalink|Usa|Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{jalink|Buzen|Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{jalink|Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{jalink|Kōnin|Q1373070}} 14 ({{jalink|823|Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{jalink|official shrine}} in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}})'{{jalink|Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{jalink|Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}}, leaving {{jalink|ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{jalink|Minamata|Q386110}}, {{jalink|Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308}}, and {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{jalink|mountain worship|Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{jalink|Shimazu clan|Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{jalink|Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} of {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475|Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{jalink|Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 36 ({{jalink|1961|Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 1 ({{jalink|1989|Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

shrine.jpg|270px|Kashikuri Shrine]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}
{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建 = Trad. {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}}
Trad. {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|March 4|Q2396}} / {{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and its former rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} In addition, {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{jalink|sessha/massha|Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{jalink|Usa|Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{jalink|Buzen|Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{jalink|Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{jalink|Kōnin|Q1373070}} 14 ({{jalink|823|Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{jalink|official shrine}} in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}})'{{jalink|Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{jalink|Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}}, leaving {{jalink|ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{jalink|Minamata|Q386110}}, {{jalink|Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308}}, and {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{jalink|mountain worship|Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{jalink|Shimazu clan|Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{jalink|Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} of {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475|Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{jalink|Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 36 ({{jalink|1961|Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 1 ({{jalink|1989|Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Kashikuri-jinja}} [[JA:加紫久利神社]] [[SIMPLE:Kashikuri Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q11398800}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kashikuri Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kashikuri shrine.jpg|270px|Kashikuri Shrine]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}
{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建 = Trad. {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}}
Trad. {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|March 4|Q2396}} / {{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and its former rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} In addition, {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{jalink|sessha/massha|Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{jalink|Usa|Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{jalink|Buzen|Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{jalink|Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{jalink|Kōnin|Q1373070}} 14 ({{jalink|823|Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{jalink|official shrine}} in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}})'{{jalink|Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{jalink|Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}}, leaving {{jalink|ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{jalink|Minamata|Q386110}}, {{jalink|Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308}}, and {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{jalink|mountain worship|Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{jalink|Shimazu clan|Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{jalink|Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} of {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475|Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{jalink|Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 36 ({{jalink|1961|Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 1 ({{jalink|1989|Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}
{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建 = Trad. {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}}
Trad. {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|March 4|Q2396}} / {{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and its former rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} In addition, {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{jalink|sessha/massha|Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{jalink|Usa|Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{jalink|Buzen|Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{jalink|Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{jalink|Kōnin|Q1373070}} 14 ({{jalink|823|Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{jalink|official shrine}} in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}})'{{jalink|Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{jalink|Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}}, leaving {{jalink|ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{jalink|Minamata|Q386110}}, {{jalink|Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308}}, and {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{jalink|mountain worship|Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{jalink|Shimazu clan|Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{jalink|Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} of {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475|Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{jalink|Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 36 ({{jalink|1961|Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 1 ({{jalink|1989|Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}

{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建 = Trad. {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}}
Trad. {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|March 4|Q2396}} / {{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and its former rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} In addition, {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{jalink|sessha/massha|Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{jalink|Usa|Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{jalink|Buzen|Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{jalink|Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{jalink|Kōnin|Q1373070}} 14 ({{jalink|823|Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{jalink|official shrine}} in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}})'{{jalink|Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{jalink|Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}}, leaving {{jalink|ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{jalink|Minamata|Q386110}}, {{jalink|Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308}}, and {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{jalink|mountain worship|Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{jalink|Shimazu clan|Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{jalink|Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} of {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475|Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{jalink|Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 36 ({{jalink|1961|Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 1 ({{jalink|1989|Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}
{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建 = Trad. {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}}
Trad. {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|March 4|Q2396}} / {{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and its former rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} In addition, {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{jalink|sessha/massha|Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{jalink|Usa|Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{jalink|Buzen|Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{jalink|Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{jalink|Kōnin|Q1373070}} 14 ({{jalink|823|Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{jalink|official shrine}} in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}})'{{jalink|Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{jalink|Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}}, leaving {{jalink|ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{jalink|Minamata|Q386110}}, {{jalink|Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308}}, and {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{jalink|mountain worship|Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{jalink|Shimazu clan|Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{jalink|Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} of {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475|Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{jalink|Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 36 ({{jalink|1961|Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 1 ({{jalink|1989|Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

shrine.jpg|270px|Kashikuri Shrine]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}{{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}
{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建 = Trad. {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}}
Trad. {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|March 4|Q2396}} / {{jalink|November 23|Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and its former rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} *{{jalink|Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{jalink|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}} In addition, {{jalink|Inari deity|Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{jalink|sessha/massha|Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{jalink|Age of the Gods|Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{jalink|Taihō|Q1346269|Taihō}} 2 ({{jalink|702|Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{jalink|Usa|Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{jalink|Buzen|Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{jalink|Hayato|Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{jalink|Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{jalink|Kōnin|Q1373070}} 14 ({{jalink|823|Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{jalink|Satsuma|Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{jalink|official shrine}} in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 1 ({{jalink|851|Q29966}})'{{jalink|Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635}} 2 ({{jalink|860|Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{jalink|Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379}}, leaving {{jalink|ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{jalink|Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{jalink|Minamata|Q386110}}, {{jalink|Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308}}, and {{jalink|Izumi|Q15734}}, {{jalink|Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{jalink|mountain worship|Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{jalink|Shimazu clan|Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{jalink|Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} of {{jalink|Satsuma Province|Q667475|Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 10 ({{jalink|1877|Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{jalink|Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{jalink|1880|Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 36 ({{jalink|1961|Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 1 ({{jalink|1989|Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{jalink|Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

shrine.jpg|270px|Kashikuri Shrine]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill||lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
Former {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and its former rank was {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} *{{ill||lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill||lt=Wamyōshō|UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill||lt=official shrine|UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966}})'{{ill||lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}

Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]
