Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

shrine.jpg|270px|Kashikuri Shrine]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

shrine.jpg|270px|Kashikuri Shrine]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396|1=March 4}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|1=Setsumatsusha}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861|1=Usa (宇佐)}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri|1=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745|1=Buzen}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509|1=Otomi Shrine}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944|1=823}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198|1=Hirasaki Shrine}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|1=Satsuma Province}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake|1=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110|1=Minamata, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439|1=Mountain worship}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406|1=Shimazu clan}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537|1=Shimazu Yoshitaka}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū|1=Satsuma Province}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425|1=1989}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}

{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396|1=March 4}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|1=Setsumatsusha}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861|1=Usa (宇佐)}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri|1=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745|1=Buzen}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509|1=Otomi Shrine}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944|1=823}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198|1=Hirasaki Shrine}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|1=Satsuma Province}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake|1=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110|1=Minamata, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439|1=Mountain worship}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406|1=Shimazu clan}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537|1=Shimazu Yoshitaka}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū|1=Satsuma Province}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425|1=1989}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396|1=March 4}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|1=Setsumatsusha}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861|1=Usa (宇佐)}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri|1=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745|1=Buzen}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509|1=Otomi Shrine}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944|1=823}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198|1=Hirasaki Shrine}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|1=Satsuma Province}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake|1=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110|1=Minamata, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439|1=Mountain worship}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406|1=Shimazu clan}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537|1=Shimazu Yoshitaka}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū|1=Satsuma Province}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425|1=1989}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396|1=March 4}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|1=Setsumatsusha}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861|1=Usa (宇佐)}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri|1=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745|1=Buzen}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509|1=Otomi Shrine}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944|1=823}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198|1=Hirasaki Shrine}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|1=Satsuma Province}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake|1=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110|1=Minamata, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439|1=Mountain worship}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406|1=Shimazu clan}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537|1=Shimazu Yoshitaka}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū|1=Satsuma Province}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425|1=1989}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|frame-height=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}1272 Shimosaba-chō |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 32 |緯度分 = 7 |緯度秒 = 10.7 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 20 |経度秒 =56.0 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396|1=March 4}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|1=Setsumatsusha}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861|1=Usa (宇佐)}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri|1=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745|1=Buzen}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509|1=Otomi Shrine}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944|1=823}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198|1=Hirasaki Shrine}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|1=Satsuma Province}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake|1=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110|1=Minamata, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439|1=Mountain worship}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406|1=Shimazu clan}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537|1=Shimazu Yoshitaka}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū|1=Satsuma Province}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425|1=1989}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:薩摩国|社2]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396|1=March 4}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|1=Setsumatsusha}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861|1=Usa (宇佐)}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri|1=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745|1=Buzen}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509|1=Otomi Shrine}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944|1=823}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198|1=Hirasaki Shrine}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|1=Satsuma Province}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake|1=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110|1=Minamata, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439|1=Mountain worship}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406|1=Shimazu clan}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537|1=Shimazu Yoshitaka}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū|1=Satsuma Province}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425|1=1989}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:薩摩国の式内社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:Satsuma Province]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Trad. {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}}
Trad. {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=March 4|Q2396|WD=Q2396|1=March 4}} / {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}} |神事 = Seed-sowing festival, cow dance (March 4) |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''Kashikuri Shrine''' (かしくりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shimosaba-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiri-bime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (according to shrine tradition, treated as a daughter of Amaterasu) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} In addition, {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}} is enshrined as a {{ill|lt=sessha/massha|Q1071160|WD=Q1071160|1=Setsumatsusha}}. === History === The founding date is unknown, but shrine tradition places it far back in the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods|Q3577055|WD=Q3577055|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)}} and says it has roughly a 2,000-year history. According to 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku,' however, this shrine was established in {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|WD=Q23497|1=702}}), at the same time as the founding of {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}}, by transferring the three female deities of {{ill|lt=Usa|Q22128861|WD=Q22128861|1=Usa (宇佐)}} to the foothills of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Mount Kashikuri|1=Mount Kashikuri}} on the Higo border. It further states this was done by Kashi no Kimi Watari, thought to be from Kashikuyari in Kamimike District, {{ill|lt=Buzen|Q411745|WD=Q411745|1=Buzen}}, who took part in the Taihō 2 campaign against the {{ill|lt=Hayato|Q1151764|WD=Q1151764|1=Hayato people}} and enshrined the deity of his homeland's {{ill|lt=Ōtomi Shrine|Q11434509|WD=Q11434509|1=Otomi Shrine}}. Kashi no Kimi, rewarded for service, was ordered to guard the Higo border after the war and settled here, naming the area Kashikuri after his home Kashikuyari. At the time, Buzen Kashikuyari became Suee-gō under local-administration reforms, and Satsuma Kashikuri likewise changed to a same-reading district name "Kariya-gō" due to main/branch-family relations, preserving the name in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}}''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture'. In {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|WD=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 14 ({{ill|lt=823|Q29944|WD=Q29944|1=823}}), Kashikuri Shrine was fixed as the great shrine for {{ill|lt=Satsuma|Q227481|WD=Q227481|1=Satsuma}} among Japan's 66 provinces. It was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=official shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1 ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}})'{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=UNKNOWN}}', Ninju 1, 6th month, day 17., and in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) was raised in rank from Junior Fifth Lower to Junior Fifth Upper'{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', Jōgan 2, 3rd month, day 20.. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' it appears together with {{ill|lt=Hirakiki Shrine|Q3136198|WD=Q3136198|1=Hirasaki Shrine}} and is one of only two shrines listed for {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|1=Satsuma Province}}. For this reason, it later came to be called "Satsuma Ninomiya" (in Satsuma, after the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, two shrines were both treated as {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}, leaving {{ill|lt=ichinomiya#ninomiya/sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} unclear). The "Mount Kashikuri" in shrine tradition is said to be an old name of {{ill|lt=Yahazudake (Minamata/Izumi)|Q11584247|WD=Q11584247|lt=Yahazudake|1=Yahazudake}}, straddling the border of {{ill|lt=Minamata|Q386110|WD=Q386110|1=Minamata, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Izumi|Q15734|WD=Q15734|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|Q15701|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. At the summit is '''Yakoshi Shrine''', administered by this shrine. Because Yakoshi Shrine's annual festival is also March 4, the same as this shrine's spring festival, some relationship is suggested, though no clear tradition survives. It is also possible this shrine originally began as {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|WD=Q93697439|1=Mountain worship}} with Mount Kashikuri as its {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. As a historically important shrine linked to the founding of Satsuma, it was strongly revered by the {{ill|lt=Shimazu clan|Q1061406|WD=Q1061406|1=Shimazu clan}}, rulers of this region. The 21st head {{ill|lt=Shimazu Yoshitaka|Q11246537|WD=Q11246537|1=Shimazu Yoshitaka}} had it recognized as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Satsuma Province|Q667475|WD=Q667475|lt=Sasshū|1=Satsuma Province}} and undertook construction of shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}), it was completely burned in the {{ill|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|Q714820|WD=Q714820|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, losing not only shrine buildings but also inherited treasures. Buildings were rebuilt in Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}}), but later land development greatly reduced the precincts, and by the postwar period the shrine buildings even faced collapse. === Shrine buildings === The current buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 36 ({{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696|1=1961}}) through local donations, and renovated in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1989|Q2425|WD=Q2425|1=1989}}). === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * 'Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 46 Kagoshima Prefecture', {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|Q1054765|WD=Q1054765|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1983 === External links === * {{Instagram|kasikuri_shrine}} * [https://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/shrine-search/area-hokusatsu/出水町/502/ Kashikuri Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashikuri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Satsuma Province]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:Satsuma Province]] [[Category:出水市の建築物]] [[Category:出水市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]
